response.justification: The candidate is an experienced and versatile senior software engineer, but his background is almost entirely in web, full-stack, and product development. The role demands SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his experience. His brief low-latency systems work at Sociomantic and C/C++ work at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level hardware performance architecture expertise.
strengths: Long, diverse software engineering career (15+ years) exceeding the raw experience requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers, millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure, plus recent AI-agent tooling (MCP) familiarity
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, system architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator, deep learning, or GPU architecture background
Strong general engineering skills with some low-latency systems work
